title: Bank "fraud" call asking for verification code
raw_description: A caller claimed to be from my bank's fraud team and said my account would be locked unless I "verified" a one-time code. They asked me to read the code back over the phone. I hung up and called the number on my card; the bank said they did not place the call.
recommended_checklist: Do not share one-time codes with anyone.
Call back using a trusted number (card/app), not the inbound call.
Enable MFA and review recent logins and transactions.
ai_summary: Impersonation phone call attempted to capture a one-time verification code; bank confirmed it was not legitimate.

title: Package delivery text with tracking link
raw_description: Received a text saying a delivery could not be completed and asking me to confirm my address via a shortened link. The message used urgent language and threatened a return to sender.
recommended_checklist: Avoid clicking shortened links in unexpected texts.
Check shipment status by typing the carrier site manually.
Report the message as spam and delete it.
ai_summary: Likely SMS delivery scam using a shortened link to lure address/payment confirmation.

title: Job offer over messaging app requesting gift cards
raw_description: A "recruiter" offered a remote job and then asked me to buy gift cards to "verify" my identity and send the codes. The pay rate was unusually high and the account would not do a video call.
recommended_checklist: Never pay or buy gift cards to secure a job offer.
Verify recruiters through official company channels.
Report the account on the messaging platform.
ai_summary: Classic fake job scam: high pay, no verification, and a request for gift card codes.

title: Email about overdue utility bill with attachment
raw_description: An email claimed my utility account was overdue and included a PDF attachment named "invoice_important.pdf". The sender address looked slightly off and the message pushed immediate payment.
recommended_checklist: Do not open unexpected attachments from unknown senders.
Log into the utility portal directly to confirm billing.
Run an antivirus scan if the attachment was opened.
ai_summary: Suspicious utility-bill email with an attachment; treated as phishing and removed.

title: Password reset email for account I don't use
raw_description: Got a password reset email for a social media account I haven't used in years. The email looked legitimate, but I didn't request it. I did not click the button.
recommended_checklist: Do not click reset links you didn't request.
Navigate to the service directly and change the password there.
Enable MFA and check account recovery settings.
ai_summary: Unrequested password reset email could indicate account probing; avoid links and verify via the official site.

title: Fake "school alert" voicemail asking for callback
raw_description: A voicemail claimed there was an urgent school closure and asked me to call back a number to confirm details. The message didn't name the school and used a generic recording.
recommended_checklist: Do not call back unknown numbers from generic urgent messages.
Check official school communication channels directly.
Block the number and report the voicemail as spam.
ai_summary: Likely robocall using an urgent "school alert" pretext to drive callbacks.

title: Community forum link claiming "crime map" update
raw_description: A post in a neighborhood forum linked to an external site promising a "real-time crime map" and asked users to "log in with email" to view details. The site looked hastily made.
recommended_checklist: Avoid logging into unknown sites linked from forum posts.
Use official city resources for public safety information.
Report suspicious posts to moderators.
ai_summary: Suspicious external "crime map" link prompting logins; treat as potential credential-harvesting.

title: Unexpected "account locked" pop-up while browsing
raw_description: While reading news on my phone, a full-screen pop-up claimed my device was infected and my account was locked, telling me to call a number. Closing the tab made it disappear.
recommended_checklist: Do not call numbers shown in browser pop-ups.
Close the tab/app; clear browser data if it reappears.
Update the device and run a trusted security scan.
ai_summary: Scareware pop-up attempted to push a phone call; safely dismissed and cleared.

title: Car break-ins reported near trail parking lot
raw_description: Several cars in the trail parking lot had windows smashed overnight. No injuries reported. People mentioned valuables left visible in the back seat.
recommended_checklist: Remove valuables and bags from sight before parking.
Park in well-lit areas when possible.
Report suspicious activity to local non-emergency services.
ai_summary: Verified cluster of overnight vehicle break-ins at the Lakeside trail parking area.

title: Suspicious vehicle slowly circling residential block
raw_description: A dark SUV drove slowly through the block multiple times in the afternoon and stopped briefly near driveways. No direct contact occurred, but it felt unusual.
recommended_checklist: Note distinguishing details (time, location, description).
Ensure doors/windows are locked and outdoor lights work.
Contact non-emergency services if the behavior repeats.
ai_summary: Unverified report of a vehicle repeatedly circling the Riverside block; monitor and document patterns.

title: Door-to-door "utilities" impersonation attempt
raw_description: Two people wearing generic safety vests claimed they needed to check inside the home due to a "gas leak" in the area. They could not provide identification or a service order number.
recommended_checklist: Do not let unknown visitors inside without verified ID.
Call the utility company using a trusted number to confirm.
Report suspicious visitors to local non-emergency services.
ai_summary: Unverified door-to-door impersonation pretexted as a utility safety check; visitors lacked proper ID.

title: Streetlight outage reported on Maplewood block
raw_description: Several streetlights are out along the block, making the sidewalk and intersections much darker at night. No incidents reported yet, but visibility is poor.
recommended_checklist: Use well-lit routes when walking at night.
Consider temporary motion lighting at home entrances.
Submit/track a city repair request if not already filed.
ai_summary: Verified streetlight outage affecting nighttime visibility in Maplewood; repair request logged.

title: Bike theft attempt near apartment racks
raw_description: Someone was seen trying to cut a bike lock near the apartment racks around dusk. They left when approached. No theft confirmed.
recommended_checklist: Use a hardened U-lock and secure both frame and wheel.
Avoid leaving bikes outside overnight when possible.
Report suspicious activity to property management.
ai_summary: Unverified attempted bike-lock cutting near Downtown apartment racks; increase locking precautions.

title: Noisy near-duplicate: "heard glass" late night
raw_description: Around 1am I heard what might have been glass breaking somewhere nearby, but I could not see anything from my window. Posting in case others heard it too.
recommended_checklist: If you see an active incident, call local emergency services.
Share only general location details (no exact addresses).
Check in with neighbors if you feel safe doing so.
ai_summary: Low-information report of a possible loud noise; included for pattern awareness but not confirmation.

title: Power outage followed by unknown door checks
raw_description: During a brief power outage, someone walked down the street checking door handles. A neighbor saw them leave quickly when porch lights came back on.
recommended_checklist: Confirm exterior doors/windows are secured during outages.
Use battery lighting and consider motion lights.
Report suspicious behavior to non-emergency services.
ai_summary: Unverified report: door-handle checking observed during a short power outage in South Park.

title: Loose dog reported near playground
raw_description: A medium-sized dog without a leash was seen running near the playground after school. No bites reported, but a few kids were startled.
recommended_checklist: Keep children close and avoid approaching unknown animals.
Contact animal control or park staff if the dog returns.
Share general location details to help reunite with owner.
ai_summary: Loose dog near Eastwood playground was reported and later handled; continue to monitor for reoccurrence.

title: Router admin password still set to default
raw_description: While helping a neighbor reset their Wi‑Fi, we noticed the router admin page was still using the default username/password printed on the device. The Wi‑Fi password was also shared widely.
recommended_checklist: Change the router admin password (not just Wi‑Fi).
Update firmware and enable automatic updates if available.
Use WPA2/WPA3 with a strong Wi‑Fi passphrase.
ai_summary: Verified weak router setup: default admin credentials and broadly shared Wi‑Fi password increased compromise risk.

title: Unknown device connected to home Wi‑Fi
raw_description: Router device list showed a new device name I did not recognize connected overnight. I changed the Wi‑Fi password and kicked devices, but I'm not sure how it got on.
recommended_checklist: Change Wi‑Fi password and disconnect existing sessions.
Update router firmware and disable remote admin access.
Check for reused passwords on key accounts.
ai_summary: Potential unauthorized Wi‑Fi access: unknown device observed; password changed but follow-up hardening advised.

title: Phishing email disguised as "router firmware update"
raw_description: Email claimed my router needed an urgent firmware update and included a link to download a file. It referenced my ISP by name but came from a free email address.
recommended_checklist: Only update firmware via the router admin page or vendor site.
Do not download executables from email links.
If you clicked, run a malware scan and change key passwords.
ai_summary: Suspicious firmware-update email likely intended to deliver malware; updates should be done through trusted channels.

title: Password reuse concern after "haveibeenpwned" alert
raw_description: A friend told me my email appeared in a breach notification site. I have reused the same password on a few accounts. No sign of takeover yet.
recommended_checklist: Change passwords on your most important accounts first.
Use a password manager to create unique passwords.
Enable MFA on email, banking, and primary logins.
ai_summary: Breach exposure plus password reuse increases risk; prioritize unique passwords and MFA even without confirmed takeover.

title: Smart doorbell repeatedly going offline
raw_description: My smart doorbell has been disconnecting from Wi‑Fi at night for the last few days. It might be signal issues, but I'm worried it could be interference.
recommended_checklist: Update device firmware and reboot the router.
Check Wi‑Fi signal strength and change to a less congested channel.
Review router logs for repeated disconnects.
ai_summary: Intermittent IoT disconnects may be benign; update firmware and check Wi‑Fi channel/signal before assuming interference.

title: Neighbor received "tech support" remote access request
raw_description: A pop-up told my neighbor to call a number for "Microsoft support". The person on the phone asked to install remote-access software. They stopped before installing anything.
recommended_checklist: Do not install remote-access tools at a stranger's request.
Close the browser and run a malware scan.
Consider updating the browser and enabling pop-up blocking.
ai_summary: Resolved tech-support scam attempt: remote-access installation was requested but prevented.

title: Two-factor prompts arriving unexpectedly
raw_description: I received multiple MFA push notifications overnight for an account I use daily. I denied them and changed my password this morning.
recommended_checklist: Change the account password and revoke active sessions.
Review sign-in history and recovery options.
Prefer phishing-resistant MFA methods when available.
ai_summary: Verified account-probing: repeated MFA push prompts were denied; password change and session review completed.

title: Noisy near-duplicate: "Wi‑Fi named like mine"
raw_description: I noticed a Wi‑Fi network name very similar to mine appear nearby (same name with "_5G" added). It could be normal, but I want to flag it.
recommended_checklist: Confirm your router's SSID and Wi‑Fi password in the admin panel.
Use WPA2/WPA3 and disable WPS if possible.
Avoid connecting to lookalike networks; forget unknown networks.
ai_summary: Possible lookalike Wi‑Fi SSID observed; verify your network settings and avoid connecting to unknown SSIDs.
